g.text: The user is asking me to execute the skill as described in the system prompt. This is an "example skill" that
- When invoked, briefly explain what skills are and how they help
This seems straightforward - I should just provide a brief explanation about skills without needing to use any tools.
Skills are specialized capabilities that can be invoked to perform specific tasks or provide focused expertise. They
help by organizing functionality into modular, reusable components that can be easily called when needed. Skills
streamline workflows by encapsulating common operations and domain knowledge into self-contained units.
> What is the example skill about?
The user is asking about the example skill. Looking at the command message, there's a skill definition provided that
explains what this example skill is about. Let me read it again:
The skill is titled "Example Skill" and it's described as "a demonstration skill that shows how skill invocation
The instructions say: "When invoked, briefly explain what skills are and how they help. Keep the response under 3
So this is a demonstration/example skill that shows how skills work, and when it's invoked, it should briefly explain
what skills are and how they help in under 3 sentences.
The example skill is a demonstration that shows how skill invocation works. When invoked, it briefly explains what
skills are and how they help, keeping the response under 3 sentences.
